calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kelsey Quaranto & John Harbour for Harry Marvin.

Ms. Quaranto stated that the examiner cited 12408 regarding what to do if the decedent reappears and that they are not certain regarding it. The Court stated that it thought they were referred to 12401 and to file a petition to administer the estate. Ms. Quaranto stated that they are not looking to establish probate, that there are not a lot of funds, that the funds are being used to pay for the life insurance policy and that they are looking to obtain a death certificate. The Court stated that the issue is if there is enough reliable evidence. Mr. Harbour stated that they are representing the father who is in North Carolina, that there is an ongoing criminal investigation, that closure is needed, that on December 28, 2016 there is a hearing for the first and final accounting and to close the conservatorship, that after they receive the order for fact of death they will be able to move forward with a small estate affidavit and that they have provided all of the evidence from the Police Department. The Court asked what the costs would be if they proceeded with probate. Mr. Harbour stated that the cost would be time, that it would take another year, that the assets have been safe under Police authority and that this would allow for the life insurance policy to move forward. Ms. Quaranto stated that this is not a large estate to administer, that there are only two life insurance policies and that they have named beneficiaries, that there is only personal property to distribute and that it is below the $150,000.00 threshold. Mr. Harbour stated that there is only $3-$4,000.00 left, that there are no opposing parties and that there is no evidence of foul play. The Court took the petition to establish fact of death under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
